component.name: Mint Mechanic
component.summary: The maintained tune-up tool for Linux Mint

description.p: Mint Mechanic is a modern, Cinnamon-native successor to Stacer: a live system dashboard with animated CPU, RAM and disk gauges plus a GPU dial, married to the maintenance actions Linux Mint does not ship.
It deliberately stays out of the system-information lane that Mint already covers, and works in the action and optimization space instead.

ul.li: Dashboard with live analog gauges for CPU, RAM, disk and GPU, plus network throughput, load average and uptime
Services — enable or disable systemd services with live status
Startup — manage your per-user autostart entries
Cleaner — reclaim space from the APT cache, orphaned packages, thumbnails, Trash and old system logs
Uninstaller — search your manually-installed packages and remove them
Streamline — export your installed package set as a portable profile, or import one to see what is missing
A persistent GO/NO-GO health strip covering disk, failed units, pending updates and reboot-required

description.p: Mint Mechanic never runs as root. Reads happen as your own user, and each individual action that changes the system elevates on its own through polkit. Anything that removes packages shows you what APT would actually remove — including everything pulled in through dependencies — and asks you to confirm, refusing outright if the removal would take your desktop, login manager or graphics driver with it.

screenshot.caption: The dashboard, with live CPU, RAM, disk and GPU gauges
Reclaiming disk space with the cleaner
Exporting a portable package profile with Streamline

description.p: Published to a PPA so updates arrive through the normal system update process, and added AppStream metadata so the app lists in software centres. Packaging moved to a standard Debian source package.
Adds a removal preview and confirmation to the Uninstaller, and moves the shared removal guard into the single package seam so the Cleaner and Uninstaller cannot diverge.
Safety fix: the Cleaner's orphaned-package task now shows APT's real removal cascade, requires confirmation, and refuses removals that would take session-critical packages.
Security fix for package-name handling when importing a Streamline profile, corrected path quoting in the Cleaner, and the addition of a test suite and continuous integration.
